jobs.title: Staff Product Manager - Key Accounts
jobs.jd_full:
At Too Good To Go, we have an ambitious mission: to inspire and empower everyone to fight food waste together.

More than 1⁄3 of all food produced in the world is wasted. And that has a huge impact on the health of our planet. 10% of greenhouse gas emissions come from food waste and loss.

Through our marketplace app, we connect businesses that have unsold, surplus food, with consumers who can buy and enjoy it at ⅓ of retail value. We are a certified B Corporation with a mission to empower everyone to take action against food waste. Alongside our marketplace app, we create educational tools, explore new business solutions, and influence legislation to help reduce food waste.

We’re growing fast: Our community of 120 million registered users and 180,000 active partners across 20 countries, have together already saved over 500 million meals from going to waste

In 2023, Too Good To Go was recognised on Fortune’s acclaimed ‘Change the World’ list, named as one of Deloitte’s ‘Best Managed Companies’ and won a coveted ‘Cultural Impact Award’ at the annual Apple App Store Awards.

Why This Role - and Why London

We are establishing our first product team in London and we want to be clear about why. Two things make London the right place for this team.

First, the partners. The UK is home to some of our most strategically important Key Accounts: major grocery chains, national coffee shop operators, and fast casual restaurant groups. The best way to build great products for them is to be close to them to understand their businesses from the inside, not through a quarterly call.

Second, the people who drive those relationships at TGTG. London is where a lot of our most senior operational and commercial leadership sits P&L owners who are deeply invested in the Key Account agenda, alongside the Key Account managers and commercial teams working those partnerships every day. Being in the room with these people, not coordinating with them across time zones, is what makes the difference.

Your Mission

As Staff Product Manager for Key Accounts, you will lead our effort to prove and grow the value Too Good To Go creates for our most important enterprise partners.

Key Accounts — the grocery chains, coffee shop operators, and fast casual groups that partner with us at scale — represent one of our most significant growth levers. They also have the highest expectations. They need to see the ROI of our partnership clearly, and they need to feel it growing. Your job is to make both happen: to demonstrate the value TGTG already creates for them, and to keep expanding it — developing new solutions and features that deepen what partners get from working with us. You do that by understanding their businesses deeply, building products that address their real needs, and working with our commercial organisation to turn that value into lasting relationships.

This is a new team, built from scratch, with a clear mandate. It requires someone who can define the strategy, earn the buy-in, and drive the execution — independently, with energy, and with conviction.

The Team

You will be the founding PM in London, operating as an individual contributor at Staff level. You will report directly to our VP of Product based in Copenhagen. The role is designed for someone who does not need daily direction — who can set the agenda, manage up effectively, and make strong decisions with incomplete information.

Responsibilities

As Staff Product Manager for Key Accounts, you will:

  • Define the product strategy for Key Accounts, developing clear OKRs and roadmaps that reflect both the commercial opportunity and a deep understanding of what our enterprise partners actually need.
  • Go deep with partners. Spend real time with our Key Account partners — grocery groups, coffee chains, fast casual operators — to understand their business model, how they measure success, and what they see as genuine value. Translate that understanding into product decisions.
  • Partner with commercial leadership to develop a shared roadmap. Drive alignment with speed and transparency. Build genuine buy-in — not just agreement on paper, but real momentum behind a shared direction.
  • Prove and grow the value. Build the measurement frameworks that demonstrate TGTG’s impact for Key Accounts — and develop new solutions and features that expand what partners recognise as valuable in the partnership. Both sides of this matter: credibility with existing impact, and ambition for what comes next.
  • Drive execution across a cross-functional team, orchestrating designers, engineers, data analysts, and commercial partners around a clear product vision.
  • Leverage data and insights — commercial data, partner feedback, market signals — to sharpen strategy, identify new opportunities, and communicate progress and performance.
  • Manage upward and across with confidence. Represent your domain to senior leadership in Copenhagen, make the case for resources and priority, and handle pushback with rigour and composure.
  • Be the founding product presence in London. Shape what good product thinking looks like in this market, and set a standard others will want to match.

What We Are Looking For

Experience and background:

  • Multi-year experience in a Senior or Staff Product Management role — within a marketplace, platform, or technology business.
  • Grocery, coffee, or fast casual experience is a genuine differentiator. We are looking for someone who understands how these businesses operate from the inside: how they measure performance (footfall, basket size, shrink, sell-through, ROI), what their operating pressures are, and what they actually care about in a partnership. This is not a hard requirement, but candidates who have it will stand out significantly.
  • Proven track record of delivering transformational business outcomes — not just shipping product, but demonstrably moving the metrics that matter to partners and to the business.

How you work:

  • You are comfortable - and genuinely thrive - working independently, with a remote manager and a high degree of autonomy. You set your own agenda and follow through on it.
  • You are an exceptional stakeholder partner. You build trust with senior commercial and operational leaders, create genuine alignment rather than managed agreement, and know how to drive a roadmap with energy and conviction even when there is friction.
  • You bring a rigorous analytical mind. You can build business cases, define measurement frameworks, and use data to make the case - internally and externally.
  • You move fast. You do not wait for perfect information. You are decisive, transparent about your reasoning, and quick to adjust.
  • You bring energy. The kind that is infectious - that makes cross-functional teams want to follow your lead, and that makes partners feel like they are in good hands.
  • You have a growth mindset. You are always looking for the next level - in the product, in the strategy, and in yourself.
  • You are genuinely fun to work with. You bring your authentic self, you care about the mission, and you know how to succeed as part of a team.

Our values:

  • We Win Together
  • We Raise the Bar
  • We Keep It Simple
  • We Build A Legacy
  • We Care

What we have to offer

  • A rare opportunity to work in a social impact company (and certified B Corporation!) where you can see real and tangible impact in your role.
  • Working alongside an international community of users, partners and 1,200+ colleagues across 20 countries that are on the same important mission.
  • Personal and professional development opportunities in a fast-paced scale-up environment.
  • An inclusive company culture where you can bring your authentic self to work
  • A strong, values-driven team culture where we celebrate successes and socialise with colleagues that care.

Benefits

  • WORK FLEXIBLY:

    • Enjoy hybrid working from our great offices, at home or abroad.
    • Extra parental leave and days off beyond local legislation and the option to take an extra week of unpaid leave
    • Health insurance and pension plans (subject to country of employment).
    • Additional days off for significant life events

  • CELEBRATE & SOCIALISE

    • Regular social events like summer and winter parties.
    • Coffee, snacks and fully-equipped kitchens.
    • Get to know our community with a monthly free Surprise Bag
    • Paid volunteer time through our Shareback volunteering programme
    • Women in the Workplace, P.R.I.D.E., People of Colour and Functionally Diverse Employee Resource Groups

How to apply

  • We take recruitment very seriously, so please carefully read everything we have written above. Please also check our website and international media in order to get a good overview of Too Good To Go.
  • Submit your CV and Cover letter in English.
  • Please note that we only accept applications coming through our platform. No CV or Cover Letter will be accepted by email or LinkedIn direct messaging.

A Movement for Everyone

We want to inspire and empower everyone to fight food waste together. With that mission, it’s only natural that we want to build a diverse and inclusive team of highly capable individuals who are passionate about doing things in a better way. We strongly believe we all excel and are more creative when we’re allowed to be ourselves, and we’re committed to a culture where all of us belong.

We are an equal opportunity employer and all employment is decided on the basis of qualifications, merit and business need. If you need reasonable accommodation at any point in the application or interview process, please let us know.

jobs.title: Senior Program Manager / Account Manager
jobs.jd_full:
Company Description

CapTech is a team of master builders, creators, and problem solvers who help clients grow efficient, successful businesses. We unite diverse skills and perspectives to transform how data, systems, and ingenuity enable each client to advance what’s possible in a changing world.

As perceptive partners, our U.S-based consultants find inspiration in the unknown and enjoy getting our hands dirty solving our clients’ myriad of challenges. Across industries and business goals, we fuse technical depth and analytical prowess with creative savvy to move clients forward. This drive helps each organization use technology, management, and insight to turn ideas into action. Together, we create outcomes that exceed the expected — which is one of the reasons we’ve been on the Inc. 500⁄5000 list for over a decade.

Job Description

As a Program Manager / Account Manager with CapTech, your focus will be helping our clients deliver results against some of their most complex business and technology initiatives. The Program Manager / Account Manager will partner with and ensure the long-term success of our clients. Different projects could challenge you to succeed as a highly consultative senior management advisor or to drive highly visible technical projects based on your planning, execution, and client relationship management skills. The Program Manager / Account Manager will develop long-term relationships with our client stakeholders and ensure successful service delivery.

Specific responsibilities for the Program Manager / Account Manager position include:

  • Establish collaborative partnership with key stakeholders to assess and recommend optimal business and technical solutions.
  • Lead cross-functional delivery teams to deliver impactful business outcomes.
  • Develop integrated plans to establish, monitor, manage and report key milestones and deliverables.
  • Support the triage and resolution of critical program or project issues and scope changes throughout the project phases.
  • Develop a trusted advisor relationship with key client stakeholders and executive sponsors
  • Build and maintain strong, long-lasting customer relationships.
  • Perform hands-on project work, including analysis, design, process engineering, and change management, as needed by the project.
  • Advocates for digital accessibility and inclusive design practices in all projects and client interactions.

Qualifications

Specific qualifications for the Program Manager / Account Manager position include:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Computer Science, MIS or equivalent combination of education and experience
  • PMP certification preferred.
  • Proven account management or other relevant experience.
  • Minimum 8 years of experience in project or program management role, preferably with full agile development life cycle experience.
  • Demonstrated track record of successful ability to manage large and complex consulting engagements.
  • Ability to assess client needs, identify omissions and gaps, and design optimal solution.
  • Creative thinking and seeking innovative solutions to complex technical/business problems.
  • Ability to establish immediate credibility with clients, build consensus, and achieve goals through influence.
  • Excellent communication skills, listening, negotiation and presentation skills.

Additional Information

We want everyone at CapTech to be able to envision a lasting and rewarding career here, which is why we offer a variety of career paths based on your skills and passions.  You decide where and how you want to develop, and we help get you there with customizable career progression and a comprehensive benefits package to support you along the way.  Alongside our suite of traditional benefits encompassing generous PTO, health coverage, disability insurance, paid family leave and more, we’ve launched extended benefits to help meet our employees’ needs.

  • CapTech is committed to providing a flexible work environment and helping our employees achieve a work-life balance that suits their individual needs. Employees must be available to work onsite in a client location or a CapTech office as requested. We allow CapTech employees to work remotely when compatible with CapTech and client needs.
  • Learning & Development – Programs offering certification and tuition support, digital on-demand learning courses, mentorship, and skill development paths
  • Modern Health –A mental health and well-being platform that provides 1:1 care, group support sessions, and self-serve resources to support employees and their families through life’s ups and downs
  • Carrot Fertility –Inclusive fertility and family-forming coverage for all paths to parenthood – including adoption, surrogacy, fertility treatments, pregnancy, and more – and opportunities for employer-sponsored funds to help pay for care
  • Fringe –A company paid stipend program for personalized lifestyle benefits, allowing employees to choose benefits that matter most to them – ranging from vendors like Netflix, Spotify, and GrubHub to services like student loan repayment, travel, fitness, and more
  • Employee Resource Groups – Employee-led committees that embrace and incorporate diversity and inclusion into our day-to-day operations
  • Philanthropic Partnerships – Opportunities to engage in partnerships and pro-bono projects that support our communities.
  • 401(k) Matching – Generous matching and no vesting period to help you continue to build financial wellness

CapTech is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering a culture of equality, inclusion and fairness — each foundational to our core values.  We strive to create a diverse environment where each employee is encouraged to bring their unique ideas, backgrounds and experiences to the workplace. For more information about our Diversity, Inclusion and Belonging efforts, click HERE.

CapTech supports Equal Pay for all. In addition, in the State of Colorado, we are committed to Equal Pay for ALL in accordance with the Colorado Equal Pay for Equal Work Act. The base pay range for this role is: $130,000 - $160,000.

At this time, CapTech cannot transfer nor sponsor a work visa for this position. Applicants must be authorized to work directly for any employer in the United States without visa sponsorship.

jobs.title: Steel Construction Project Manager
jobs.jd_full:
Steel Construction Project Manager

Employment Type: Full-Time | Direct Hire

Compensation: Starts at $65,000 annually and may be higher depending on experience.

Location: Hanover, PA or Richmond, VA | Remote work available with periodic travel to project sites throughout Pennsylvania, Maryland, and Virginia

Schedule: Monday–Friday, 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM

Additional hours and occasional Saturdays may be required based on project schedules.

Position Summary

We are seeking a Steel Construction Project Manager to oversee the successful execution of commercial and industrial steel projects from project kickoff through final completion. This role is responsible for coordinating engineering, detailing, fabrication, delivery, installation, and project closeout activities while ensuring projects remain on schedule, within budget, and aligned with quality and safety standards.

The ideal candidate has experience managing structural steel or miscellaneous metals projects and is comfortable collaborating with clients, subcontractors, fabricators, and field personnel throughout the project lifecycle.

Primary Responsibilities

Project Planning & Execution

  • Manage multiple steel construction projects through all phases, including preconstruction, fabrication, installation, and closeout.
  • Develop and maintain project schedules to support engineering, submittals, procurement, production, shipping, field installation, and final turnover activities.
  • Review contract documents, drawings, and specifications to verify project scope, material requirements, and execution plans.
  • Monitor project progress and proactively identify opportunities to improve efficiency and project outcomes.

Engineering & Coordination

  • Coordinate detailing, surveying, and project engineering activities.
  • Review shop drawings and technical documentation for completeness and accuracy.
  • Collaborate with design teams and field personnel to resolve project challenges and maintain schedule commitments.
  • Participate in Building Information Modeling (BIM) coordination efforts, including clash detection and trade coordination.

Contract & Financial Management

  • Prepare and process project documentation including submittals, purchase orders, subcontract agreements, professional service agreements, and contract modifications.
  • Develop schedules of values and oversee monthly billing submissions.
  • Prepare, negotiate, and track change orders, project revisions, credits, and pricing proposals.
  • Monitor project costs and support profitability objectives throughout project execution.

Client & Vendor Relations

  • Build and maintain productive relationships with owners, contractors, suppliers, subcontractors, and other project stakeholders.
  • Coordinate material deliveries, installation sequencing, and site logistics with trade partners.
  • Facilitate communication among all project participants to minimize delays and maintain project momentum.
  • Address project issues promptly and implement corrective actions when necessary.

Documentation & Compliance

  • Maintain complete and organized project records in both digital and physical formats.
  • Support quality control initiatives and ensure compliance with contractual requirements.
  • Issue performance or corrective action notices to subcontractors when required.
  • Promote adherence to company safety expectations and industry best practices.

Qualifications

Required

  • Minimum of 4 years of project management experience within construction, steel fabrication, steel erection, or related industries.
  • Experience managing commercial or industrial construction projects.
  • Strong knowledge of steel construction practices, materials, methods, and installation procedures.
  • Proficiency with Microsoft Office applications.
  • Valid U.S. driver’s license.
  • Strong organizational, communication, and problem-solving skills.
  • Ability to perform project-related calculations and conduct field verification when necessary.

Preferred

  • Associate’s or Bachelor’s degree in Construction Management, Engineering, Project Management, or a related field.
  • Experience with scheduling platforms such as Microsoft Project or Primavera.
  • Familiarity with Tekla, Fabtrol, or similar construction management and detailing software.
  • Knowledge of project cost control, quality assurance, and safety management practices.
  • Experience participating in BIM coordination and clash detection processes.

Work Environment

  • Combination of office, remote, and construction site work environments.
  • Regular exposure to active construction conditions, including dust, welding operations, paint fumes, elevated noise levels, and moving equipment.
  • Occasional exposure to electrical systems and mechanical hazards.
  • Approximately 15% travel to project locations throughout the region.
  • Mileage reimbursement available for approved business travel.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

jobs.title: Legal & Compliance Project Manager
jobs.jd_full:
NinjaHoldings was founded in 2017 by a team seeking to revolutionize the way everyday Americans interact with financial services. Through our CreditNinja and NinjaCard brands, we empower people overlooked by traditional financial institutions to take control of their finances via a full suite of digital banking and lending products, providing incentives and rewards along the way as we guide them on a path to financial improvement. Through our EDGE brand, we help companies better understand their customers by offering a package of bespoke underwriting, fraud detection, and analytics services. With offices in Chicago, Miami, and around the world through the power of remote work, we are a lean and innovative team always seeking like-minded talent to join us in our fight to disrupt consumer finance.

Job Summary

We are hiring a Compliance & Legal Project Manager to own and drive the execution of initiatives originating from two separate departments, Compliance and Legal. This role is designed for candidates with strong product or management backgrounds who are comfortable decomposing complex requirements, coordinating cross-functional work, and driving delivery in regulated environments.

The role will focus on program management and execution, not regulatory interpretation or legal decision-making. The goal is to ensure compliance and legal-driven work moves forward in a structure, visible way without placing execution burden on department leadership.

Location and Work Model

Compliance leadership is based in Sarasota. Legal leadership is based in Chicago. This role will report through Compliance, with a strong partnership to Legal regardless of the candidate’s primary location. Preference for candidates based in Chicago, IL or Sarasota, FL.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Own and manage the Compliance Program Build Jira space, which serves as the central PM and coordination space for compliance and legal initiatives, ensuring work is routed to the correct Jira space and tracked appropriately
  • Intake and prioritize initiatives originating from Compliance and Legal
  • own cross-functional Epics and drive them from definition through completion
  • Translate compliance and legal requirements into clear execution plans
  • Coordinate execution across Compliance, Legal, Product, Engineering, Operations, and external partners
  • Ensure required legal and bank reviews and approvals are obtained, documented, and tracked
  • Identify risks, dependencies, and blockers early and escalate when needed
  • Provide clear status updates and delivery visibility to Compliance and Legal leadership and ensure initiatives close with proper validation, approvals, and evidence retained

Ideal Candidate Will Have:

  • 5+ years of experience as a Program Manager, Product Manager, Project Manager, or similar role
  • Demonstrated experience delivering complex, cross-team initiatives
  • Experience working in regulated industries strongly preferred
  • Comfort working closely with Engineering, Product, Compliance, and Legal teams
  • Strong experience using Jira to manage cross-functional work, including Epics, stories and linked workflows across multiple projects
  • Demonstrated ability to manage a program build or initiative board that coordinates work executed by other teams
  • Familiarity with software development lifecycles, releases processes, and change management protocols
  • Experience operating in controlled or regulated change environments
  • Strong organizational and prioritization skills

Additional Pluses:

  • Experience supporting Compliance, Legal, Audit, or Risk teams
  • Fintech, banking, payments, or consumer finance experience
  • Familiarity with data validation or reporting workflows, including the ability to interpret reports or query outputs
  • Experience coordinating work involving external partners or regulators

Benefits:

  • Competitive salary and benefits package
  • Flexible, remote work
  • Fun, fast-paced work environment
  • Dynamic start-up culture
  • Ability to make an immediate impact in a growth stage company
  • Convenient downtown Chicago office located in the heart of the city
  • Equal opportunity employer

IMPORTANT NOTICE:

Please carefully review communications to ensure that they are from the official Breezy applicant tracking platform (@ breezy-mail.com ) or an official NinjaHoldings brand email: @ ninjaholdings.com , @ creditninja.com , @ ninjacard.com , or @ edgescore.com. If you have been contacted regarding a job opening at NinjaHoldings from any other email address, including similar email variations, this is NOT a trusted source. We recommend that you refrain from responding to suspicious emails and file a complaint with the FBI’s Internet Crime Complaint Center (IC3) at https://www.ic3.gov . For questions or to confirm the authenticity of a communication, please email hr @ninjaholdings.com .
